title: Supply Planner
description: Guide the supply planning transformation: from shared vision and process design through data governance, system readiness, training, and lasting adoption.

sections.title: Shared Vision
sections.description: Understand why the transformation is happening, what success looks like, and how to align the organisation behind the change.

sections.title: Operating Model
sections.description: How S&OP and S&OE work — the governance layer, meeting cadence, and how plans move from decision to execution.

sections.title: Roles & Responsibilities
sections.description: Who does what in the planning organisation — team structure, role definitions, and the accountability framework that makes it work.

sections.title: Process Design
sections.description: How the planning processes are structured — decision frameworks, escalation paths, exception handling, and service level design.

sections.title: Data Requirements
sections.description: What data the plan depends on — the data model, where data comes from, how it flows in, and what happens when it's wrong.

sections.title: Data Governance
sections.description: How data quality is owned, measured, and improved — from defining good enough to assigning accountability and building confidence.

sections.title: System Design
sections.description: How the technology is architected — the data model, system interfaces, batch processing, and how ERP and planning software integrate.

sections.title: Training
sections.description: How to build planning capability — assessing gaps, sequencing the learning journey, and delivering training that transfers to the job.

sections.title: Adoption
sections.description: Making the new way of working permanent — understanding why adoption fails and how to embed lasting behavioural change.

sections.title: Advanced Planning
sections.description: Scenario analysis, what-if simulation, and the policy decisions — safety stock, allocation, prioritisation — that sharpen the supply plan.

sections.title: Continuous Improvement
sections.description: How to sustain and improve performance — KPI frameworks, exception management, data quality cycles, and scenario governance.
